title: Examples of Opposite Sides That Can Both Be True  Â‰ 1. You can want to change and be doing the best you can, AND still need to do better, try harder, and be more motivated to change.

pages.text:
152
From DBT Skills Training Handouts and Worksheets, Second Edition by Marsha M. Linehan. Copyright 2015 by Marsha M. Linehan. Permission
to photocopy this handout is granted to purchasers of this book for personal use only (see copyright page for details).
Interpersonal Effectiveness Handout 16a
Examples of Opposite Sides That Can Both Be True

Â‰   1.	 You can want to change and be doing the best you can, AND still need to do better, try
harder, and be more motivated to change.

Â‰   2.	You are tough AND you are gentle.

Â‰   3.	You can be independent AND also want help. (You can allow somebody else to be
independent and also give them help.)

Â‰   4.	You can want to be alone AND also want to be connected to others.

Â‰   5.	You can share some things with others AND also keep some things private.

Â‰   6.	You can be by yourself AND still be connected to others.

Â‰   7.	 You can be with others AND be lonely.

Â‰   8.	You can be a misfit in one group AND fit in perfectly in another group. (A tulip in a rose
garden can also be a tulip in a tulip garden.)

Â‰   9.	You can accept yourself the way you are AND still want to change. (You can accept others
as they are AND still want them to change.)

Â‰ 10.	 At times you need to both control AND tolerate your emotions.

Â‰ 11.	 You may have a valid reason for believing what you believe, AND you may still be wrong or
incorrect.

Â‰ 12.	Someone may have valid reasons for wanting something from you, AND you may have valid
reasons for saying no.

Â‰ 13.	The day can be sunny, AND it can rain.

Â‰ 14.	 You can be mad at somebody AND also love and respect the person.

Â‰ 15.	 (You can be mad at yourself AND also love and respect yourself.)

Â‰ 16.	You can have a disagreement with somebody AND also be friends.

Â‰ 17.	 You can disagree with the rules AND also follow the rules.

Â‰ 18.	You can understand why somebody is feeling or behaving in a certain way, AND also
disagree with his or her behavior and ask that it be changed.

Â‰ 19.	 Others:

Interpersonal Effectiveness Handout 16a
Examples of Opposite Sides That Can Both Be True

Â‰   1.	 You can want to change and be doing the best you can, AND still need to do better, try
harder, and be more motivated to change.

Â‰   2.	You are tough AND you are gentle.

Â‰   3.	You can be independent AND also want help. (You can allow somebody else to be
independent and also give them help.)

Â‰   4.	You can want to be alone AND also want to be connected to others.

Â‰   5.	You can share some things with others AND also keep some things private.

Â‰   6.	You can be by yourself AND still be connected to others.

Â‰   7.	 You can be with others AND be lonely.

Â‰   8.	You can be a misfit in one group AND fit in perfectly in another group. (A tulip in a rose
garden can also be a tulip in a tulip garden.)

Â‰   9.	You can accept yourself the way you are AND still want to change. (You can accept others
as they are AND still want them to change.)

Â‰ 10.	 At times you need to both control AND tolerate your emotions.

Â‰ 11.	 You may have a valid reason for believing what you believe, AND you may still be wrong or
incorrect.

Â‰ 12.	Someone may have valid reasons for wanting something from you, AND you may have valid
reasons for saying no.

Â‰ 13.	The day can be sunny, AND it can rain.

Â‰ 14.	 You can be mad at somebody AND also love and respect the person.

Â‰ 15.	 (You can be mad at yourself AND also love and respect yourself.)

Â‰ 16.	You can have a disagreement with somebody AND also be friends.

Â‰ 17.	 You can disagree with the rules AND also follow the rules.

Â‰ 18.	You can understand why somebody is feeling or behaving in a certain way, AND also
disagree with his or her behavior and ask that it be changed.

Â‰ 19.	 Others:

ourspace_shared_life_filter.title: Examples of Opposite Sides That Can Both Be True  Â‰ 1. You can want to change and be doing the best you can, AND still need to do better, try harder, and be more motivated to change.
ourspace_shared_life_filter.shared_facing_summary: Use this to protect both people: needs are real, boundaries are kind, and a pause is not abandonment when repair is named.
tiny_steps: Name the ask in one sentence.
Agree on a return time or next check-in.

flare_day_versions.minimum: One breath, one label, one safe message, or one checkbox.
flare_day_versions.medium: Two to five minutes with everything reachable from bed/chair.
flare_day_versions.full: Use the original worksheet/handout when both bodies and brains have capacity.

accessibility_modifications: Use text/chat if speech is hard.
Include caregiver capacity as part of the plan, not an afterthought.
relationship_and_caregiver_notes: William's needs and Jasper's capacity both count.
Ask before helping; ask before escalating; name the smallest next step.
Pause means pause, not abandonment. I am coming back. I care about us. I need a smaller next step.
What is needed, what capacity exists, what can be smaller, and what can wait?
identity_and_trauma_filter: Use William's masculine language and Jasper's they/them language.
Use consent-based, non-shaming, body-neutral language unless a person has explicitly chosen a term.
Do not turn symptoms, dysphoria, trauma responses, clutter, fatigue, or emotional intensity into moral failure.
avoid_or_adjust: Do not use skills to win, corner, threaten leaving, or make either person prove pain or disability.
